old_exact: 1The Estate also sued Todd and Darcie Grager. Those claims were dismissed by the district court, and
new_exact: 1 The Estate also sued Todd and Darcie Grager. Those claims were dismissed by the district court, and
source_quote:
and were raised



1 The Estate also sued Todd and Darcie Grager. Those claims were dismissed by the district court, and
evidence: PDF pdftotext output shows '1 The Estate' (with space after footnote number) at line 147. DB shows '1The Estate' (missing space). This is a clear ingestion/transcription defect where the space after the footnote marker was lost. The character sequence occurs exactly once in the DB text.

source_quote: G.J.E.P., Sr., father,                           Respondent and Appellant
evidence: The DB line 36 has a leading space before 'Respondent and Appellant' that does not appear in the PDF. The first caption (DB lines 8-24) shows the same phrase without a leading space on line 16, confirming this is an inconsistent ingestion artifact. The PDF shows both captions use the same format without leading spaces.
note: Whitespace/formatting defect: leading space inconsistently added to second caption only. Removing the space makes this line consistent with the first caption (line 16) and the PDF source.

evidence: The DB line 42 has a leading space before 'Respondents' that does not appear in the PDF. The first caption (DB lines 8-24) shows the same phrase without a leading space on line 22, confirming this is an inconsistent ingestion artifact. The PDF shows both captions use the same format without leading spaces.
note: Whitespace/formatting defect: leading space inconsistently added to second caption only. Removing the space makes this line consistent with the first caption (line 22) and the PDF source.

source_quote: 1 During the second oral argument in this matter, we were informed
evidence: Footnote reference marker '1' is directly attached to word 'During' in DB text but separated by a space in authoritative PDF source. The PDF clearly shows '1 During' (with space) at the footnote location. This is a transcription/ingestion artifact where a space was lost between the footnote marker and the following text.

[¶3] We conclude the district court did not abuse its discretion
note: Extra blank line removed between citation introduction and final citation in list. Parallel to Issue 1.

evidence: PDF line 78 shows regular space (U+0020) between 'Gerald W.' and 'VandeWalle'. DB contains narrow no-break space (U+202F). Character-by-character analysis confirms the PDF has U+0020 (SPACE) where DB has U+202F (NARROW NO-BREAK SPACE). This is a single-occurrence Unicode ingestion artifact.
note: The narrow no-break space (U+202F) appears to be a PDF-to-text conversion artifact. The authoritative PDF source clearly shows a regular space character.

source_quote:
Rentz v. BNSF Ry. Co., 2020 ND 254, ¶ 12,
952 N.W.2d 47
evidence: Trailing space character (0x20) before newline in DB is not present in authoritative PDF source. All three instances are in factual citations and can be verified visually against PDF.
note: Mechanical whitespace error: space before line break in citations. Occurs in Rentz v. BNSF citation at [¶7].

evidence: Trailing space before newline in DB. PDF shows no space. Verified by byte-level comparison and full-text search.
note: Mechanical whitespace error: space before line break in citation. Occurs in Rittenour v. Gibson citation at [¶19].
